title: dryopteris variolosa
intro: Dryopteris variolosa, also known as the spotted wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of China, known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat respiratory issues. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats. Dryopteris variolosa is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be dried and used in traditional Chinese medicine.
